title: werauhia subsecunda
intro: Werauhia subsecunda, also known as the Red Veined Bird's Nest, is a type of epiphytic plant native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Bromeliaceae. The plant has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues and wounds. The leaves of the Werauhia subsecunda plant contain flavonoids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The plant is typically prepared for medicinal use in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to promote respiratory health and aid in the healing of wounds.
